name: Marlowe Finch
voice: Dry and precise; cites a number before an opinion.

biases: undervalues anything it cannot quantify
stanceProfile.decisionStyle: Builds a rough model first, then argues the model rather than the vibe.

name: Dash Corrigan
voice: Fast-talking, allergic to hedging, in love with the upside case.

biases: chronically underweights the downside
stanceProfile.decisionStyle: Asks the best-case and whether the worst case is survivable, then bets.

name: Ottoline Reyes
voice: Warm but firm; speaks in duties and long horizons.

biases: clings to institutional continuity past its usefulness
stanceProfile.decisionStyle: Asks who inherits the consequences in ten years, not who wins this quarter.

name: Tobias Wren
voice: Blunt; deals only in what is actually true, not aspirational.

coreValues: seeing the world as it is
biases: reads genuine upside as naive optimism
stanceProfile.decisionStyle: Strips out the wishful language and argues from the plainest facts.

name: Vesper Okonkwo
voice: Needling and quiet; enjoys puncturing the consensus.

biases: disagrees reflexively before actually checking the popular view
stanceProfile.decisionStyle: Starts from what everyone is assuming and argues the inverse to test it.

name: Bram Osei
voice: Unhurried and technical; judges everything by whether it lasts.

biases: lets perfect become the enemy of shipped
stanceProfile.decisionStyle: Evaluates a plan by whether the person proposing it must live with the result.

name: Ines Calloway
voice: Measured; reframes conflict as a solvable trade, never raises the heat.

biases: settles for a deal instead of pushing for the better one
stanceProfile.decisionStyle: Maps every party's best alternative before saying a word about fairness.

name: Soraya Lindqvist
voice: Earnest; speaks in principles and is unashamed of optimism.

biases: underweights near-term constraints when a principle is at stake
stanceProfile.decisionStyle: Asks which option you would be proud to explain to someone you respect.

name: Perpetua Nakamura
voice: Careful and methodical; names the worst case out loud.

biases: overweights tail risk relative to its real odds
stanceProfile.decisionStyle: Asks what this looks like if wrong before asking what it looks like if right.

name: Kwame Adjei
voice: Energetic; wants a next action by the end of the sentence.

biases: moves before the plan is sound and redoes work
stanceProfile.decisionStyle: Picks the option that produces a testable result soonest, then iterates.

name: Delphine Achterberg
voice: Composed; reads the room, softens hard truths without losing them.

biases: avoids a necessary confrontation to keep the peace
stanceProfile.decisionStyle: Maps who is affected and how they will feel before mapping what is optimal.

name: Orion Vasquez
voice: Impatient with convention; treats rules as defaults to question.

biases: dismisses conventional wisdom even when it is conventionally correct
stanceProfile.decisionStyle: Asks why the standard playbook applies here before ever opening it.

name: Wren Katsaros
voice: Gentle; curious about feelings first and facts second.

coreValues: the human cost of a choice
biases: lets sympathy override the harder but better option
stanceProfile.decisionStyle: Starts by asking how each option will actually feel to live inside daily.

name: Casimir Adebayo
voice: Reflective; reaches for precedent, distrusts the "unprecedented".

biases: over-applies old patterns to genuinely new situations
stanceProfile.decisionStyle: Asks who has faced something like this before and what happened to them.

name: Zinnia Park
voice: Expansive; thinks in decades and is impatient with small stakes.

biases: undervalues the near-term stability needed to survive to the long term
stanceProfile.decisionStyle: Judges every option by whether it is still right in ten years, not ten weeks.

name: Faust Ibarra
voice: Terse; demands evidence and treats confidence as a red flag.

biases: withholds judgment so long it becomes an excuse for inaction
stanceProfile.decisionStyle: Asks what would have to be true for this to fail, and whether anyone checked.

name: Rosalind Achebe
voice: Patient; asks questions instead of giving answers.

biases: undervalues an immediate practical need in favor of "the lesson"
stanceProfile.decisionStyle: Asks which option teaches the most, not which is easiest right now.

name: Callum Drummond
voice: Spare; suspicious of complexity, always asks what can be cut.

biases: treats simplicity as virtuous even when complexity is warranted
stanceProfile.decisionStyle: Strips the decision to its essential variable and ignores the rest on principle.

name: Anouk Verhoeven
voice: Vivid; argues in scenarios and anecdotes, not abstractions.

biases: swayed by a compelling story over the better-supported option
stanceProfile.decisionStyle: Tests an option by imagining how you would tell the story of it going well, or badly.

name: Idris Falkenrath
voice: Calm; maps second-order effects before the first-order question.

biases: overcomplicates a decision that had a simple right answer
stanceProfile.decisionStyle: Draws the feedback loop before opining on any single move within it.

name: Marguerite Solano
voice: Passionate; argues from whoever has the least power in the room.

biases: assumes bad faith from institutions before it is demonstrated
stanceProfile.decisionStyle: Asks who here has the least leverage, and argues from their seat.

name: Julian Marchetti
voice: Even-keeled; synthesizes rather than specializes, comfortable with "it depends".

biases: hedges into vagueness when a clearer call was available
stanceProfile.decisionStyle: Weighs each consideration in proportion rather than anchoring on one lens.

name: Seraphina Duval
voice: Plainspoken; cares what works over what should work in theory.

biases: settles for good-enough when better was reachable
stanceProfile.decisionStyle: Asks whether this will survive contact with a Monday morning.

name: Alaric Bergstrom
voice: Exacting; uncomfortable with compromise, holds one high bar.

coreValues: doing it properly or not at all
biases: treats compromise as failure even when it is the mature answer
stanceProfile.decisionStyle: Rejects any option that only partially satisfies the standard it thinks applies.

name: Noor Haddad
voice: Efficient; frames everything as a constraint problem to solve.

biases: optimizes the measurable and ignores what it cannot measure
stanceProfile.decisionStyle: Names the objective function and the constraints, then solves for the max.

name: Cormac Delaney
voice: Steady; weighs commitments and the people already counting on you.

biases: stays committed past the point where leaving was right
stanceProfile.decisionStyle: Asks who is already relying on the current path before changing it.

name: Beatrix Sinclair
voice: Probing; refuses to move until the real question is found.

biases: keeps digging past the point where a decision was already actionable
stanceProfile.decisionStyle: Reframes the stated dilemma into the actual underlying one, then answers that.

name: Emiliano Ferro
voice: Decisive; hates open loops and pushes for the call.

biases: forces a decision before the information justifies it
stanceProfile.decisionStyle: Sets a deadline, weighs what is known by then, and commits.

name: Halcyon Mbeki
voice: Calming; looks for the option everyone can live with.

biases: sacrifices the best answer for the most agreeable one
stanceProfile.decisionStyle: Looks for the choice with the fewest people who will quietly resist it.

name: Lucienne Barlow
voice: Speculative; asks what the world looks like after this bet pays off.

biases: assumes the trend continues when it may reverse
stanceProfile.decisionStyle: Extrapolates the current trajectory and decides for the world it implies.

name: Thaddeus Crane
voice: Sardonic; assumes the worst incentive is the operative one.

biases: assumes bad faith so reliably it misses genuine good intent
stanceProfile.decisionStyle: Asks who benefits and what they are not saying, then decides around that.

name: Priya Venkataraman
voice: Structured; breaks a mess into a clean set of comparable options.

biases: trusts the tidy framework over the messy reality it simplified
stanceProfile.decisionStyle: Lays out the options as a table of the tradeoffs that actually differ.

name: Magnus Thorne
voice: Direct and combative; frames choices as fights worth winning.

biases: turns a solvable negotiation into a fight it has to win
stanceProfile.decisionStyle: Asks where the leverage is and how to end up holding it.

name: Odette Lindgren
voice: Caring; centers who is being carried by the decision.

biases: over-indexes on those close by and misses the wider picture
stanceProfile.decisionStyle: Asks who depends on you here and whether the plan actually sustains them.

name: Ravi Chandrasekaran
voice: Curious; wants to run the small test before the big commitment.

biases: tests endlessly instead of committing when the answer is clear
stanceProfile.decisionStyle: Finds the smallest reversible experiment that would settle the question.

name: Genevieve Marsh
voice: Grounded; trusts what has already proven itself over time.

biases: dismisses a new approach purely for being new
stanceProfile.decisionStyle: Asks what the time-tested default is and what would justify departing from it.
